mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Make it configurable which claim is user id (currently = email)
mission.description: Make it configurable which claim is user id (currently = email). Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
According to https://github.com/pusher/oauth2_proxy/blob/master/providers/oidc.go#L208 an id token must contain an email. But some of our tokens don't have (the users use their phone number instead), which makes the proxy unusable for us.

steps.title: Configure the --user-id-claim flag
steps.description:
Update your oauth2-proxy deployment to use the `--user-id-claim` flag, which allows you to specify which OIDC token claim should be used as the user identifier instead of the default `email` claim.

For a Kubernetes Deployment, edit the container args:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment oauth2-proxy -n oauth2-proxy
```
Add the flag to the args section:
```yaml
containers:
  - name: oauth2-proxy
    args:
      - --user-id-claim=sub
      # Or use any custom claim, e.g.:
      # - --user-id-claim=preferred_username
      # - --user-id-claim=phone_number
```
This is useful when your identity provider does not include an email claim in the ID token (e.g., users authenticate with phone numbers).

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Restart the oauth2-proxy pods and verify the new claim is being used:
```bash
kubectl rollout restart deployment oauth2-proxy -n oauth2-proxy
kubectl rollout status deployment oauth2-proxy -n oauth2-proxy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy -n oauth2-proxy --tail=20
```
Authenticate through the proxy and confirm that the `X-Forwarded-User` header now contains the value from your configured claim instead of the email.

resolution.summary: Added the --user-id-claim configuration flag that allows specifying which OIDC token claim to use as the user identifier. Previously hardcoded to 'email', users can now set it to 'sub', 'preferred_username', 'phone_number', or any custom claim. Internally, SessionState.Email was renamed to UserID with backward-compatible session decoding.
codeSnippets:
# Use the 'sub' claim as user ID instead of email
oauth2-proxy --user-id-claim=sub --provider=oidc --oidc-issuer-url=https://issuer.example.com

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
